steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the cylinder volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder is $$V = \pi r^2 h$$ where $r$ is the radius and $h$ is the height. We are given $r = 13$ m and $h = 10$ m.

steps.title: Substitute values and compute
steps.content:
Substitute into the formula: $$V = \pi (13)^2 (10) = \pi \cdot 169 \cdot 10$$
Compute $169 \times 10 = 1,690$

Therefore, $V = 1,690\pi$ m³. That matches Choice D — $1,690\pi$.
